question.text: The STT rate applicable on the sale of futures contracts in the equity derivatives segment is:

answer.text: 0.02% of the price at which futures are traded, payable by the seller
0.10% of the traded value, payable by both buyer and seller
0.125% of the settlement price, payable by the buyer
No STT is applicable on futures contracts

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the Finance Act, STT on sale of futures is 0.02% of the traded price, payable by the seller. For options: sale = 0.10% on premium (seller), exercise = 0.125% on settlement price (purchaser). **The Trap:** 0.02% applies to futures only. Don't confuse with options rates (0.10% and 0.125%). Also, STT on physically settled derivatives follows delivery-based equity rates (0.1% by both parties). **Cross-Exam Context:** STT rates are frequently tested across Series VIII, I, and XIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Futures: 0.02% (seller). Options sale: 0.10% (seller on premium). Options exercise: 0.125% (buyer on settlement price).
